revision.text:
'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|ja|鈴鹿郡|de|Suzuka-gun|zh|鈴鹿郡|lt=Suzuka district|WD=Q278828}} || {{ill|Tsubaki Grand Shrine|lt=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|en|Tsubaki Grand Shrine|ja|椿大神社|commons|Category:Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|fa|معبد بزرگ تسوباکی|fr|Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro|id|Kuil Agung Tsubaki|ru|Цубаки-оками-ясиро|zh|椿大神社|WD=Q3541617}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || {{ill|ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya}} || — || {{coord|34.964299|136.452029|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181257 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q3541617}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Suzuka, Mie, Japan}} {{See also|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsubaki Grand Shrine
椿大神社 | image = Tsubaki-shrine.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tsubaki Grand Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|52|N|136|27|06|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] | founded_by = c. [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] | established = c.3 BC | date_destroyed = | location =1871 Otabi, Yamamoto-chō, Suzuka-shi, Mie-ken 519-0315 | website = {{Official|1=https://tsubaki.or.jp/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = October 11 | leadership = Yukiyasu Yamamoto }} {{nihongo|'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine'''|椿大神社|Tsubaki Okamiyashiro}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Yamamoto neighborhood of the city of [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the two shrines which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Ise Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 11.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tsubaki Grand Shrine https://tsubaki.or.jp/about/ are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]'''| 猿田彦大神|}}, leader of the ''[[kunitsukami]]'' and patron of martial arts such as [[aikido]]. * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}}, grandson of [[Amaterasu]] and great-grandfather [[Emperor Jimmu]] * {{nihongo|'''Takuhatachichi-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 栲幡千々姫命|}}, ''kami'' of clothing, weaving, mother of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto'''| 天之鈿女命|}}, ''kami'' of dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts; wife of Sarutahiko * {{nihongo|'''Konohanasakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 木花咲耶姫命 |}}, ''kami'' of life; wife of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Gyōman-Daimyōjin'''| 行満大明神 |}}, ''kami'' of training, study, business; ancestor of the ''kannushi'' Yamamoto clan ==History== The origins of Tsubaki Grand Shrine are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, the shrine's legend states that it was founded in the year 3 BCE{{cite web|title=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376|accessdate=22 December 2017|archive-date=23 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180223214403/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376/|url-status=dead}} during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Suinin]] by the order of [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] on the site of the grave of Sarutahiko. It first appears in historical documentation in an entry dated 748 in the {{nihongo|''Daianji garan engi nami rukishizaichō''|大安寺伽藍縁起並流記資財帳 |}} records of [[Daian-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. It is listed as a major shrine in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time, although this is contested by [[Tsubaki Jinja]], (also in Suzuka), which claims that it is the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki''.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Further according to the shrine's legend, a descendant of Sarutahiko, Gyōman Daimyōjin, was the founder of [[Shugendō]] and this the shrine was a center of the Shugendō religion in the Heian and [[Kamakura period]]s. The current chief priest claims to be the 97th generation descendant of Gyōman Daimyōjin. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a County shrine in 1871 and was promoted to a Prefectural shrine in 1927 under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1935, it was designed as the head shrine for approximately 2000 shrines in the country dedicated to Sarutahiko, although only a small fraction of these shrines actually have any connection to this shrine. One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} *[https://tsubaki.or.jp/ Official home page] * [http://tsubakishrine.org/kaminomichi/index.html Kami no Michi: The Way of the Kami]—The Life And Thought of a Shinto Priest, by Guji Yukitaka Yamamoto (96th priest of Tsubaki Grand Shrine) *[https://www.kankomie.or.jp/report/detail_379.htmlMie Prefecture Official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Province]] [[Category:Suzuka, Mie]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] revision.text:
'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|ja|鈴鹿郡|de|Suzuka-gun|zh|鈴鹿郡|lt=Suzuka district|WD=Q278828}} || {{ill|Tsubaki Grand Shrine|lt=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|en|Tsubaki Grand Shrine|ja|椿大神社|commons|Category:Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|fa|معبد بزرگ تسوباکی|fr|Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro|id|Kuil Agung Tsubaki|ru|Цубаки-оками-ясиро|zh|椿大神社|WD=Q3541617}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || {{ill|ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya}} || — || {{coord|34.964299|136.452029|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181257 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q3541617}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Suzuka, Mie, Japan}} {{See also|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsubaki Grand Shrine
椿大神社 | image = Tsubaki-shrine.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tsubaki Grand Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|52|N|136|27|06|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] | founded_by = c. [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] | established = c.3 BC | date_destroyed = | location =1871 Otabi, Yamamoto-chō, Suzuka-shi, Mie-ken 519-0315 | website = {{Official|1=https://tsubaki.or.jp/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = October 11 | leadership = Yukiyasu Yamamoto }} {{nihongo|'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine'''|椿大神社|Tsubaki Okamiyashiro}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Yamamoto neighborhood of the city of [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the two shrines which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Ise Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 11.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tsubaki Grand Shrine https://tsubaki.or.jp/about/ are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]'''| 猿田彦大神|}}, leader of the ''[[kunitsukami]]'' and patron of martial arts such as [[aikido]]. * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}}, grandson of [[Amaterasu]] and great-grandfather [[Emperor Jimmu]] * {{nihongo|'''Takuhatachichi-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 栲幡千々姫命|}}, ''kami'' of clothing, weaving, mother of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto'''| 天之鈿女命|}}, ''kami'' of dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts; wife of Sarutahiko * {{nihongo|'''Konohanasakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 木花咲耶姫命 |}}, ''kami'' of life; wife of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Gyōman-Daimyōjin'''| 行満大明神 |}}, ''kami'' of training, study, business; ancestor of the ''kannushi'' Yamamoto clan ==History== The origins of Tsubaki Grand Shrine are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, the shrine's legend states that it was founded in the year 3 BCE{{cite web|title=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376|accessdate=22 December 2017|archive-date=23 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180223214403/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376/|url-status=dead}} during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Suinin]] by the order of [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] on the site of the grave of Sarutahiko. It first appears in historical documentation in an entry dated 748 in the {{nihongo|''Daianji garan engi nami rukishizaichō''|大安寺伽藍縁起並流記資財帳 |}} records of [[Daian-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. It is listed as a major shrine in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time, although this is contested by [[Tsubaki Jinja]], (also in Suzuka), which claims that it is the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki''.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Further according to the shrine's legend, a descendant of Sarutahiko, Gyōman Daimyōjin, was the founder of [[Shugendō]] and this the shrine was a center of the Shugendō religion in the Heian and [[Kamakura period]]s. The current chief priest claims to be the 97th generation descendant of Gyōman Daimyōjin. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a County shrine in 1871 and was promoted to a Prefectural shrine in 1927 under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1935, it was designed as the head shrine for approximately 2000 shrines in the country dedicated to Sarutahiko, although only a small fraction of these shrines actually have any connection to this shrine. One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} *[https://tsubaki.or.jp/ Official home page] * [http://tsubakishrine.org/kaminomichi/index.html Kami no Michi: The Way of the Kami]—The Life And Thought of a Shinto Priest, by Guji Yukitaka Yamamoto (96th priest of Tsubaki Grand Shrine) *[https://www.kankomie.or.jp/report/detail_379.htmlMie Prefecture Official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Province]] [[Category:Suzuka, Mie]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|ja|鈴鹿郡|de|Suzuka-gun|zh|鈴鹿郡|lt=Suzuka district|WD=Q278828}} || {{ill|Tsubaki Grand Shrine|lt=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|en|Tsubaki Grand Shrine|ja|椿大神社|commons|Category:Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|fa|معبد بزرگ تسوباکی|fr|Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro|id|Kuil Agung Tsubaki|ru|Цубаки-оками-ясиро|zh|椿大神社|WD=Q3541617}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || {{ill|ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya}} || — || {{coord|34.964299|136.452029|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181257 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q3541617}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Suzuka, Mie, Japan}} {{See also|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsubaki Grand Shrine
椿大神社 | image = Tsubaki-shrine.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tsubaki Grand Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|52|N|136|27|06|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] | founded_by = c. [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] | established = c.3 BC | date_destroyed = | location =1871 Otabi, Yamamoto-chō, Suzuka-shi, Mie-ken 519-0315 | website = {{Official|1=https://tsubaki.or.jp/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = October 11 | leadership = Yukiyasu Yamamoto }} {{nihongo|'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine'''|椿大神社|Tsubaki Okamiyashiro}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Yamamoto neighborhood of the city of [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the two shrines which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Ise Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 11.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tsubaki Grand Shrine https://tsubaki.or.jp/about/ are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]'''| 猿田彦大神|}}, leader of the ''[[kunitsukami]]'' and patron of martial arts such as [[aikido]]. * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}}, grandson of [[Amaterasu]] and great-grandfather [[Emperor Jimmu]] * {{nihongo|'''Takuhatachichi-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 栲幡千々姫命|}}, ''kami'' of clothing, weaving, mother of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto'''| 天之鈿女命|}}, ''kami'' of dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts; wife of Sarutahiko * {{nihongo|'''Konohanasakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 木花咲耶姫命 |}}, ''kami'' of life; wife of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Gyōman-Daimyōjin'''| 行満大明神 |}}, ''kami'' of training, study, business; ancestor of the ''kannushi'' Yamamoto clan ==History== The origins of Tsubaki Grand Shrine are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, the shrine's legend states that it was founded in the year 3 BCE{{cite web|title=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376|accessdate=22 December 2017|archive-date=23 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180223214403/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376/|url-status=dead}} during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Suinin]] by the order of [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] on the site of the grave of Sarutahiko. It first appears in historical documentation in an entry dated 748 in the {{nihongo|''Daianji garan engi nami rukishizaichō''|大安寺伽藍縁起並流記資財帳 |}} records of [[Daian-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. It is listed as a major shrine in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time, although this is contested by [[Tsubaki Jinja]], (also in Suzuka), which claims that it is the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki''.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Further according to the shrine's legend, a descendant of Sarutahiko, Gyōman Daimyōjin, was the founder of [[Shugendō]] and this the shrine was a center of the Shugendō religion in the Heian and [[Kamakura period]]s. The current chief priest claims to be the 97th generation descendant of Gyōman Daimyōjin. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a County shrine in 1871 and was promoted to a Prefectural shrine in 1927 under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1935, it was designed as the head shrine for approximately 2000 shrines in the country dedicated to Sarutahiko, although only a small fraction of these shrines actually have any connection to this shrine. One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is one of the two shrines which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Ise Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 11.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tsubaki Grand Shrine https://tsubaki.or.jp/about/ are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]'''| 猿田彦大神|}}, leader of the ''[[kunitsukami]]'' and patron of martial arts such as [[aikido]]. * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}}, grandson of [[Amaterasu]] and great-grandfather [[Emperor Jimmu]] * {{nihongo|'''Takuhatachichi-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 栲幡千々姫命|}}, ''kami'' of clothing, weaving, mother of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto'''| 天之鈿女命|}}, ''kami'' of dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts; wife of Sarutahiko * {{nihongo|'''Konohanasakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 木花咲耶姫命 |}}, ''kami'' of life; wife of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Gyōman-Daimyōjin'''| 行満大明神 |}}, ''kami'' of training, study, business; ancestor of the ''kannushi'' Yamamoto clan ==History== The origins of Tsubaki Grand Shrine are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, the shrine's legend states that it was founded in the year 3 BCE{{cite web|title=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376|accessdate=22 December 2017|archive-date=23 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180223214403/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376/|url-status=dead}} during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Suinin]] by the order of [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] on the site of the grave of Sarutahiko. It first appears in historical documentation in an entry dated 748 in the {{nihongo|''Daianji garan engi nami rukishizaichō''|大安寺伽藍縁起並流記資財帳 |}} records of [[Daian-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. It is listed as a major shrine in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time, although this is contested by [[Tsubaki Jinja]], (also in Suzuka), which claims that it is the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki''.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Further according to the shrine's legend, a descendant of Sarutahiko, Gyōman Daimyōjin, was the founder of [[Shugendō]] and this the shrine was a center of the Shugendō religion in the Heian and [[Kamakura period]]s. The current chief priest claims to be the 97th generation descendant of Gyōman Daimyōjin. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a County shrine in 1871 and was promoted to a Prefectural shrine in 1927 under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1935, it was designed as the head shrine for approximately 2000 shrines in the country dedicated to Sarutahiko, although only a small fraction of these shrines actually have any connection to this shrine. One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. *[https://www.kankomie.or.jp/report/detail_379.htmlMie Prefecture Official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. description|Shinto shrine in Suzuka, Mie, Japan}} {{See also|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsubaki Grand Shrine
椿大神社 | image = Tsubaki-shrine.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tsubaki Grand Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|52|N|136|27|06|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] | founded_by = c. [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] | established = c.3 BC | date_destroyed = | location =1871 Otabi, Yamamoto-chō, Suzuka-shi, Mie-ken 519-0315 | website = {{Official|1=https://tsubaki.or.jp/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = October 11 | leadership = Yukiyasu Yamamoto }} {{nihongo|'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine'''|椿大神社|Tsubaki Okamiyashiro}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Yamamoto neighborhood of the city of [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the two shrines which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Ise Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 11.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tsubaki Grand Shrine https://tsubaki.or.jp/about/ are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]'''| 猿田彦大神|}}, leader of the ''[[kunitsukami]]'' and patron of martial arts such as [[aikido]]. * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}}, grandson of [[Amaterasu]] and great-grandfather [[Emperor Jimmu]] * {{nihongo|'''Takuhatachichi-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 栲幡千々姫命|}}, ''kami'' of clothing, weaving, mother of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto'''| 天之鈿女命|}}, ''kami'' of dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts; wife of Sarutahiko * {{nihongo|'''Konohanasakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 木花咲耶姫命 |}}, ''kami'' of life; wife of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Gyōman-Daimyōjin'''| 行満大明神 |}}, ''kami'' of training, study, business; ancestor of the ''kannushi'' Yamamoto clan ==History== The origins of Tsubaki Grand Shrine are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, the shrine's legend states that it was founded in the year 3 BCE{{cite web|title=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376|accessdate=22 December 2017|archive-date=23 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180223214403/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376/|url-status=dead}} during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Suinin]] by the order of [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] on the site of the grave of Sarutahiko. It first appears in historical documentation in an entry dated 748 in the {{nihongo|''Daianji garan engi nami rukishizaichō''|大安寺伽藍縁起並流記資財帳 |}} records of [[Daian-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. It is listed as a major shrine in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time, although this is contested by [[Tsubaki Jinja]], (also in Suzuka), which claims that it is the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki''.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Further according to the shrine's legend, a descendant of Sarutahiko, Gyōman Daimyōjin, was the founder of [[Shugendō]] and this the shrine was a center of the Shugendō religion in the Heian and [[Kamakura period]]s. The current chief priest claims to be the 97th generation descendant of Gyōman Daimyōjin. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a County shrine in 1871 and was promoted to a Prefectural shrine in 1927 under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1935, it was designed as the head shrine for approximately 2000 shrines in the country dedicated to Sarutahiko, although only a small fraction of these shrines actually have any connection to this shrine. One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. 椿大神社 | image = Tsubaki-shrine.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tsubaki Grand Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|52|N|136|27|06|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] | founded_by = c. [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] | established = c.3 BC | date_destroyed = | location =1871 Otabi, Yamamoto-chō, Suzuka-shi, Mie-ken 519-0315 | website = {{Official|1=https://tsubaki.or.jp/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = October 11 | leadership = Yukiyasu Yamamoto }} {{nihongo|'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine'''|椿大神社|Tsubaki Okamiyashiro}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Yamamoto neighborhood of the city of [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the two shrines which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Ise Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 11.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tsubaki Grand Shrine https://tsubaki.or.jp/about/ are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]'''| 猿田彦大神|}}, leader of the ''[[kunitsukami]]'' and patron of martial arts such as [[aikido]]. * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}}, grandson of [[Amaterasu]] and great-grandfather [[Emperor Jimmu]] * {{nihongo|'''Takuhatachichi-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 栲幡千々姫命|}}, ''kami'' of clothing, weaving, mother of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto'''| 天之鈿女命|}}, ''kami'' of dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts; wife of Sarutahiko * {{nihongo|'''Konohanasakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 木花咲耶姫命 |}}, ''kami'' of life; wife of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Gyōman-Daimyōjin'''| 行満大明神 |}}, ''kami'' of training, study, business; ancestor of the ''kannushi'' Yamamoto clan ==History== The origins of Tsubaki Grand Shrine are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, the shrine's legend states that it was founded in the year 3 BCE{{cite web|title=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376|accessdate=22 December 2017|archive-date=23 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180223214403/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376/|url-status=dead}} during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Suinin]] by the order of [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] on the site of the grave of Sarutahiko. It first appears in historical documentation in an entry dated 748 in the {{nihongo|''Daianji garan engi nami rukishizaichō''|大安寺伽藍縁起並流記資財帳 |}} records of [[Daian-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. It is listed as a major shrine in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time, although this is contested by [[Tsubaki Jinja]], (also in Suzuka), which claims that it is the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki''.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Further according to the shrine's legend, a descendant of Sarutahiko, Gyōman Daimyōjin, was the founder of [[Shugendō]] and this the shrine was a center of the Shugendō religion in the Heian and [[Kamakura period]]s. The current chief priest claims to be the 97th generation descendant of Gyōman Daimyōjin. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a County shrine in 1871 and was promoted to a Prefectural shrine in 1927 under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1935, it was designed as the head shrine for approximately 2000 shrines in the country dedicated to Sarutahiko, although only a small fraction of these shrines actually have any connection to this shrine. One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} *[https://tsubaki.or.jp/ Official home page] * One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. [[commons:Category:Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro]][[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[fa:معبد بزرگ تسوباکی]][[fr:Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro]][[id:Kuil Agung Tsubaki]][[ja:椿大神社]][[ru:Цубаки-оками-ясиро]][[zh:椿大神社]] '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|ja|鈴鹿郡|de|Suzuka-gun|zh|鈴鹿郡|lt=Suzuka district|WD=Q278828}} || {{ill|Tsubaki Grand Shrine|lt=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|en|Tsubaki Grand Shrine|ja|椿大神社|commons|Category:Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|fa|معبد بزرگ تسوباکی|fr|Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro|id|Kuil Agung Tsubaki|ru|Цубаки-оками-ясиро|zh|椿大神社|WD=Q3541617}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || {{ill|ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya}} || — || {{coord|34.964299|136.452029|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181257 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q3541617}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Suzuka, Mie, Japan}} {{See also|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsubaki Grand Shrine
椿大神社 | image = Tsubaki-shrine.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tsubaki Grand Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|52|N|136|27|06|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] | founded_by = c. [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] | established = c.3 BC | date_destroyed = | location =1871 Otabi, Yamamoto-chō, Suzuka-shi, Mie-ken 519-0315 | website = {{Official|1=https://tsubaki.or.jp/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = October 11 | leadership = Yukiyasu Yamamoto }} {{nihongo|'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine'''|椿大神社|Tsubaki Okamiyashiro}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Yamamoto neighborhood of the city of [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the two shrines which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Ise Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 11.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tsubaki Grand Shrine https://tsubaki.or.jp/about/ are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]'''| 猿田彦大神|}}, leader of the ''[[kunitsukami]]'' and patron of martial arts such as [[aikido]]. * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}}, grandson of [[Amaterasu]] and great-grandfather [[Emperor Jimmu]] * {{nihongo|'''Takuhatachichi-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 栲幡千々姫命|}}, ''kami'' of clothing, weaving, mother of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto'''| 天之鈿女命|}}, ''kami'' of dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts; wife of Sarutahiko * {{nihongo|'''Konohanasakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 木花咲耶姫命 |}}, ''kami'' of life; wife of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Gyōman-Daimyōjin'''| 行満大明神 |}}, ''kami'' of training, study, business; ancestor of the ''kannushi'' Yamamoto clan ==History== The origins of Tsubaki Grand Shrine are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, the shrine's legend states that it was founded in the year 3 BCE{{cite web|title=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376|accessdate=22 December 2017|archive-date=23 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180223214403/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376/|url-status=dead}} during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Suinin]] by the order of [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] on the site of the grave of Sarutahiko. It first appears in historical documentation in an entry dated 748 in the {{nihongo|''Daianji garan engi nami rukishizaichō''|大安寺伽藍縁起並流記資財帳 |}} records of [[Daian-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. It is listed as a major shrine in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time, although this is contested by [[Tsubaki Jinja]], (also in Suzuka), which claims that it is the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki''.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Further according to the shrine's legend, a descendant of Sarutahiko, Gyōman Daimyōjin, was the founder of [[Shugendō]] and this the shrine was a center of the Shugendō religion in the Heian and [[Kamakura period]]s. The current chief priest claims to be the 97th generation descendant of Gyōman Daimyōjin. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a County shrine in 1871 and was promoted to a Prefectural shrine in 1927 under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1935, it was designed as the head shrine for approximately 2000 shrines in the country dedicated to Sarutahiko, although only a small fraction of these shrines actually have any connection to this shrine. One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. [[commons:Category:Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro]][[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[fa:معبد بزرگ تسوباکی]][[fr:Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro]][[id:Kuil Agung Tsubaki]][[ja:椿大神社]][[ru:Цубаки-оками-ясиро]][[zh:椿大神社]] [[commons:Category:Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro]][[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[fa:معبد بزرگ تسوباکی]][[fr:Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro]][[id:Kuil Agung Tsubaki]][[ja:椿大神社]][[ru:Цубаки-оками-ясиро]][[zh:椿大神社]] '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|ja|鈴鹿郡|de|Suzuka-gun|zh|鈴鹿郡|lt=Suzuka district|WD=Q278828}} || {{ill|Tsubaki Grand Shrine|lt=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|en|Tsubaki Grand Shrine|ja|椿大神社|commons|Category:Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro|fa|معبد بزرگ تسوباکی|fr|Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro|id|Kuil Agung Tsubaki|ru|Цубаки-оками-ясиро|zh|椿大神社|WD=Q3541617}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || {{ill|ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya}} || — || {{coord|34.964299|136.452029|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181257 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q3541617}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Suzuka, Mie, Japan}} {{See also|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsubaki Grand Shrine
椿大神社 | image = Tsubaki-shrine.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tsubaki Grand Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|52|N|136|27|06|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] | founded_by = c. [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] | established = c.3 BC | date_destroyed = | location =1871 Otabi, Yamamoto-chō, Suzuka-shi, Mie-ken 519-0315 | website = {{Official|1=https://tsubaki.or.jp/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = October 11 | leadership = Yukiyasu Yamamoto }} {{nihongo|'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine'''|椿大神社|Tsubaki Okamiyashiro}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Yamamoto neighborhood of the city of [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the two shrines which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Ise Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 11.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tsubaki Grand Shrine https://tsubaki.or.jp/about/ are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]'''| 猿田彦大神|}}, leader of the ''[[kunitsukami]]'' and patron of martial arts such as [[aikido]]. * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}}, grandson of [[Amaterasu]] and great-grandfather [[Emperor Jimmu]] * {{nihongo|'''Takuhatachichi-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 栲幡千々姫命|}}, ''kami'' of clothing, weaving, mother of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto'''| 天之鈿女命|}}, ''kami'' of dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts; wife of Sarutahiko * {{nihongo|'''Konohanasakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 木花咲耶姫命 |}}, ''kami'' of life; wife of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Gyōman-Daimyōjin'''| 行満大明神 |}}, ''kami'' of training, study, business; ancestor of the ''kannushi'' Yamamoto clan ==History== The origins of Tsubaki Grand Shrine are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, the shrine's legend states that it was founded in the year 3 BCE{{cite web|title=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376|accessdate=22 December 2017|archive-date=23 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180223214403/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376/|url-status=dead}} during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Suinin]] by the order of [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] on the site of the grave of Sarutahiko. It first appears in historical documentation in an entry dated 748 in the {{nihongo|''Daianji garan engi nami rukishizaichō''|大安寺伽藍縁起並流記資財帳 |}} records of [[Daian-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. It is listed as a major shrine in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time, although this is contested by [[Tsubaki Jinja]], (also in Suzuka), which claims that it is the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki''.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Further according to the shrine's legend, a descendant of Sarutahiko, Gyōman Daimyōjin, was the founder of [[Shugendō]] and this the shrine was a center of the Shugendō religion in the Heian and [[Kamakura period]]s. The current chief priest claims to be the 97th generation descendant of Gyōman Daimyōjin. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a County shrine in 1871 and was promoted to a Prefectural shrine in 1927 under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1935, it was designed as the head shrine for approximately 2000 shrines in the country dedicated to Sarutahiko, although only a small fraction of these shrines actually have any connection to this shrine. One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. *[https://www.kankomie.or.jp/report/detail_379.htmlMie Prefecture Official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] | established = c.3 BC | date_destroyed = | location =1871 Otabi, Yamamoto-chō, Suzuka-shi, Mie-ken 519-0315 | website = {{Official|1=https://tsubaki.or.jp/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = October 11 | leadership = Yukiyasu Yamamoto }} {{nihongo|'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine'''|椿大神社|Tsubaki Okamiyashiro}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Yamamoto neighborhood of the city of [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the two shrines which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Ise Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 11.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tsubaki Grand Shrine https://tsubaki.or.jp/about/ are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]'''| 猿田彦大神|}}, leader of the ''[[kunitsukami]]'' and patron of martial arts such as [[aikido]]. * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}}, grandson of [[Amaterasu]] and great-grandfather [[Emperor Jimmu]] * {{nihongo|'''Takuhatachichi-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 栲幡千々姫命|}}, ''kami'' of clothing, weaving, mother of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto'''| 天之鈿女命|}}, ''kami'' of dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts; wife of Sarutahiko * {{nihongo|'''Konohanasakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 木花咲耶姫命 |}}, ''kami'' of life; wife of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Gyōman-Daimyōjin'''| 行満大明神 |}}, ''kami'' of training, study, business; ancestor of the ''kannushi'' Yamamoto clan ==History== The origins of Tsubaki Grand Shrine are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, the shrine's legend states that it was founded in the year 3 BCE{{cite web|title=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376|accessdate=22 December 2017|archive-date=23 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180223214403/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376/|url-status=dead}} during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Suinin]] by the order of [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] on the site of the grave of Sarutahiko. It first appears in historical documentation in an entry dated 748 in the {{nihongo|''Daianji garan engi nami rukishizaichō''|大安寺伽藍縁起並流記資財帳 |}} records of [[Daian-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. It is listed as a major shrine in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time, although this is contested by [[Tsubaki Jinja]], (also in Suzuka), which claims that it is the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki''.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Further according to the shrine's legend, a descendant of Sarutahiko, Gyōman Daimyōjin, was the founder of [[Shugendō]] and this the shrine was a center of the Shugendō religion in the Heian and [[Kamakura period]]s. The current chief priest claims to be the 97th generation descendant of Gyōman Daimyōjin. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a County shrine in 1871 and was promoted to a Prefectural shrine in 1927 under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1935, it was designed as the head shrine for approximately 2000 shrines in the country dedicated to Sarutahiko, although only a small fraction of these shrines actually have any connection to this shrine. One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} *[https://tsubaki.or.jp/ Official home page] * [http://tsubakishrine.org/kaminomichi/index.html Kami no Michi: The Way of the Kami]—The Life And Thought of a Shinto Priest, by Guji Yukitaka Yamamoto (96th priest of Tsubaki Grand Shrine) *[https://www.kankomie.or.jp/report/detail_379.htmlMie Prefecture Official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]]] [[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]]] [[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]]] [[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]]] [[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] {{Sarutahiko Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[sn:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]\ [[sn:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[sn:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[sn:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Suzuka, Mie, Japan}} {{See also|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsubaki Grand Shrine
椿大神社 | image = Tsubaki-shrine.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tsubaki Grand Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|52|N|136|27|06|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] | founded_by = c. [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] | established = c.3 BC | date_destroyed = | location =1871 Otabi, Yamamoto-chō, Suzuka-shi, Mie-ken 519-0315 | website = {{Official|1=https://tsubaki.or.jp/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = October 11 | leadership = Yukiyasu Yamamoto }} {{nihongo|'''Tsubaki Grand Shrine'''|椿大神社|Tsubaki Okamiyashiro}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Yamamoto neighborhood of the city of [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the two shrines which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Ise Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 11.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tsubaki Grand Shrine https://tsubaki.or.jp/about/ are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]'''| 猿田彦大神|}}, leader of the ''[[kunitsukami]]'' and patron of martial arts such as [[aikido]]. * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}}, grandson of [[Amaterasu]] and great-grandfather [[Emperor Jimmu]] * {{nihongo|'''Takuhatachichi-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 栲幡千々姫命|}}, ''kami'' of clothing, weaving, mother of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto'''| 天之鈿女命|}}, ''kami'' of dawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts; wife of Sarutahiko * {{nihongo|'''Konohanasakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto'''| 木花咲耶姫命 |}}, ''kami'' of life; wife of Ninigi * {{nihongo|'''Gyōman-Daimyōjin'''| 行満大明神 |}}, ''kami'' of training, study, business; ancestor of the ''kannushi'' Yamamoto clan ==History== The origins of Tsubaki Grand Shrine are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, the shrine's legend states that it was founded in the year 3 BCE{{cite web|title=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376|accessdate=22 December 2017|archive-date=23 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180223214403/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/tsu_suzuka/kanko/2376/|url-status=dead}} during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Suinin]] by the order of [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Princess Yamato]] on the site of the grave of Sarutahiko. It first appears in historical documentation in an entry dated 748 in the {{nihongo|''Daianji garan engi nami rukishizaichō''|大安寺伽藍縁起並流記資財帳 |}} records of [[Daian-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. It is listed as a major shrine in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time, although this is contested by [[Tsubaki Jinja]], (also in Suzuka), which claims that it is the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki''.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Further according to the shrine's legend, a descendant of Sarutahiko, Gyōman Daimyōjin, was the founder of [[Shugendō]] and this the shrine was a center of the Shugendō religion in the Heian and [[Kamakura period]]s. The current chief priest claims to be the 97th generation descendant of Gyōman Daimyōjin. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a County shrine in 1871 and was promoted to a Prefectural shrine in 1927 under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1935, it was designed as the head shrine for approximately 2000 shrines in the country dedicated to Sarutahiko, although only a small fraction of these shrines actually have any connection to this shrine. One the other hand, the Tsubaki Grand Shrine is the head shrine for shrined dedicated to the "Tsubaki Daimyōjin", which exist all over the country and even overseas, such as the [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]]. As Sarutahiko is revered in ''[[aikido]]'', Tsubaki has a martial arts hall for practice, as well as an archery range for the practitioners of ''[[kyūdō]]''. There is also a ''[[chashitsu]]'' (teahouse) called Reishō-an, donated by the founder of [[Panasonic]], [[Matsushita Konosuke]]. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. Behind the main shrine hall is a sacred waterfall, Kinryū Myōjin no Taki, where ''[[misogi]]'' is practiced.{{cite book|title=Shinto Shrines: A Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion|isbn=978-0824837136 |last1=Cali |first1=Joseph |last2=Dougill |first2=John |date=30 November 2012 }} The shrine is located a 45-minutes by bus from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Kansai Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 椿大神社 - 入口 石鳥居2.JPG|Entry with Torii Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|Uzume Hongū 椿大神社 - 拝殿1.JPG|Haiden 椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|sub-shrine dedicated to Matsushita Konosuke. 椿大神社 - 椿会館.JPG|Tsubaki Kaikan martial arts hall Misogi_Tsubaki-Jinja.jpg|Night misogi under a waterfall at Tsubaki Grand Shrine ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[Tsubaki Jinja]] the other Ise Ichinomiya ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe.
