Yashiro}} [[EN:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[FA:معبد بزرگ تسوباکی]] [[FR:Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro]] [[ID:Kuil Agung Tsubaki]] [[JA:椿大神社]] [[RU:Цубаки-оками-ясиро]] [[ZH:椿大神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3541617}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Grand Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsubaki-shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden of Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}} Otabi 1871, Yamamoto-cho |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|52|N|136|27|6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|kensha}}
{{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} |創建 = (tradition) 27th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |別名 = Sarutahiko Daihongu |例祭 = {{jalink|October 11|Q2927}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=52|lon_deg=136|lon_min=27|lon_sec=6|label=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|position=right|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' (つばきおおかみやしろ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and the {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}, and today it is a {{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} of {{jalink|Jinja Honcho|Q908077}}. In Mie Prefecture it ranks third in number of worshipers after {{jalink|Ise Jingu|Q687168}} and {{jalink|Futami Okitama Shrine|Q64969614}}; in 2013 it had 1,455,000 visitors{{Cite web|和書|publisher=Mie Prefecture Employment and Economy Dept., Tourism and International Bureau, Tourism Policy Division|url=http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|title=Visitor counts by tourism site category|accessdate=2015-01-25|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150124151905/http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|archivedate=2015-01-24}}. It is also called '''Sarutahiko Daihongu''', and is regarded as the head shrine of shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami. === Enshrined deities === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}}''' ; Co-enshrined deities * {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848}} - mother deity of Ninigi-no-Mikoto ; Associated deities * {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} - consort deity of Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} ; Forward seat * Gyoman Daimyojin === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 27th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}, by an oracle to {{jalink|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732}}, a shrine building was constructed near the tomb of Sarutahiko Ōkami as "the shrine of Michiwake Ōkami." The first historical appearance is in the June 17, {{jalink|748|Q23621}} ({{jalink|Tenpyo|Q1199879}} 20) ''Daian-ji Garan Engi narabi ni Ryuki Shizaicho''. This shrine is generally identified with "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' and as the ichinomiya of Ise Province. Tradition says Gyoman Daimyojin, a descendant of Sarutahiko Ōkami, founded {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} and guided {{jalink|En no Gyoja}}, and the shrine became a center of shugendo-shinto in the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}}. The current priests are said to descend from Gyoman Daimyojin. In {{jalink|1871|Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it was listed as gosha under the modern ranking system, and in {{jalink|1927|Q18110}} (Showa 2) it became kensha. In {{jalink|1935|Q18658}} (Showa 10), a survey by the Interior Ministry's Shrine Bureau designated it the head shrine ("Chigi Sarutahiko Daihongu") for about 2,000 Sarutahiko shrines nationwide. In practice, only a small number of those shrines have direct ties with this shrine. Meanwhile, shrines called "{{jalink|Tsubaki Shrine|Q11541039}}" enshrining "Tsubaki Daimyojin" exist in many regions. In {{jalink|1987|Q2429}} (Showa 62), it founded Tsubaki America Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America|Q840967}} in Washington State), beginning active promotion of {{jalink|Shinto|Q812767}} in the United States. Its treasures include a lion head said to have been dedicated by {{jalink|Kibi no Makibi|Q1063276}}, and the shrine is also considered a birthplace shrine of {{jalink|shishimai|Q713866}} lion dance. === Subsidiary shrines === === Betsugu === [[画像:Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Betsugu: Tsubakigishi Shrine]] * '''Tsubakigishi Shrine''' - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Futodama-no-Mikoto, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto * Okunomiya Iwaza (summit of {{jalink|Nyudogatake}}) - deities: Amaterasu, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Sarutahiko Ōkami * Ogishi Ōkami Shrine (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Takamimusubi-no-kami * Aishoi Shrine (Mizusawa-Noda-cho, Yokkaichi) - deity: Aishoi Daigongen * Ishi Ōkami (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Amaterasu === Massha === ==== Precinct massha ==== [[画像:椿大神社 - 土公神陵.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Doko Shinryo]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 御舟磐座2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Mifune Iwaza]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Matsushita Konosuke Shrine]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 鈴松庵2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Tea room Suzumatsu-an]] * Takayama Doko no Kami Mausoleum - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Mifune Iwaza - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Tado Shrine - deity: Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto * Gokoku Shrine - enshrined spirits of the war dead * Matsushita Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Konosuke Matsushita|Q252785|Matsushita Konosuke-no-Mikoto}} * Koryujin Shrine - deities: Golden Dragon, White Dragon, Black Dragon ==== Outside-precinct massha ==== * Tsubaki Ichinomiya Shrine (Tsubaki Ichinomiya-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Sarutahiko Ōkami * Kanno Shinmei Shrine (Kanno, Yamamoto-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Amaterasu, Sarutahiko Ōkami === Festivals === * Shishi Shin-go Kito ritual: Feb 11 - Apr 12 (once every 3 years) ** In years of Ox, Dragon, Sheep, and Dog ** First dance on Feb 11; concluding dance in precinct on Apr 12 * Setsubun Festival: day of Setsubun in February * Kinen-sai / Great purification festival: Feb 21 * Spring annual grand festival ** Spring grand festival: Apr 11 (dedication of Kyoto {{jalink|Kongo school|Q11646539}} ritual nō "Uzume") ** Kōsha grand festival: Apr 11-12 * Koryujin Shrine grand festival: early May * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya spring grand festival: May 12 * Rice-planting festival: last Sunday of May * Summer purification: Jun 30 * Summer festival: Aug 1 * Autumn annual grand festival ** Eve festival: Oct 10 ** Main shrine grand festival: Oct 11 ** Betsugu grand festival: Oct 12 * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya autumn grand festival: Nov 12 * Niiname Festival: Dec 1 * New Year's Eve great purification: Dec 31 === Visitor information === === Address === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}} Yamamoto-cho Otabi 1871 === Access === ==== Bus ==== * From {{jalink|Yokkaichi Station|Q6414379|Kintetsu Yokkaichi Station}} / {{jalink|Yokkaichi Station|Q5358995|JR Yokkaichi Station}}, take {{jalink|Mie Kotsu|Q6843516}} bus (bound for Tsubaki Grand Shrine), get off at final stop. * From Kintetsu {{jalink|Hiratacho Station|Q5769516}} / JR {{jalink|Kabase Station|Q5358774}}, take Suzuka community bus (C-BUS, Tsubaki-Hirata line loop), get off at "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" stop. ==== Private car ==== * About 5 km from {{jalink|Suzuka IC|Q11649468}} on the {{jalink|Higashi-Meihan Expressway|Q5753692}} * (ETC vehicles only) about 2 km from {{jalink|Suzuka PA Smart IC|Q11649477}} on the {{jalink|Shin-Meishin Expressway|Q7497118}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine|Q11643733}} - another shrine claiming Ichinomiya of Ise Province * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine|Q705303}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} * [http://www.tsubaki.or.jp/ Tsubaki Grand Shrine] (official website) ** {{Instagram|tsubakiokamiyashiro_official}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきおおかみやしろ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Grand Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsubaki-shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden of Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}} Otabi 1871, Yamamoto-cho |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|52|N|136|27|6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|kensha}}
{{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} |創建 = (tradition) 27th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |別名 = Sarutahiko Daihongu |例祭 = {{jalink|October 11|Q2927}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=52|lon_deg=136|lon_min=27|lon_sec=6|label=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|position=right|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' (つばきおおかみやしろ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and the {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}, and today it is a {{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} of {{jalink|Jinja Honcho|Q908077}}. In Mie Prefecture it ranks third in number of worshipers after {{jalink|Ise Jingu|Q687168}} and {{jalink|Futami Okitama Shrine|Q64969614}}; in 2013 it had 1,455,000 visitors{{Cite web|和書|publisher=Mie Prefecture Employment and Economy Dept., Tourism and International Bureau, Tourism Policy Division|url=http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|title=Visitor counts by tourism site category|accessdate=2015-01-25|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150124151905/http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|archivedate=2015-01-24}}. It is also called '''Sarutahiko Daihongu''', and is regarded as the head shrine of shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami. === Enshrined deities === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}}''' ; Co-enshrined deities * {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848}} - mother deity of Ninigi-no-Mikoto ; Associated deities * {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} - consort deity of Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} ; Forward seat * Gyoman Daimyojin === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 27th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}, by an oracle to {{jalink|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732}}, a shrine building was constructed near the tomb of Sarutahiko Ōkami as "the shrine of Michiwake Ōkami." The first historical appearance is in the June 17, {{jalink|748|Q23621}} ({{jalink|Tenpyo|Q1199879}} 20) ''Daian-ji Garan Engi narabi ni Ryuki Shizaicho''. This shrine is generally identified with "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' and as the ichinomiya of Ise Province. Tradition says Gyoman Daimyojin, a descendant of Sarutahiko Ōkami, founded {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} and guided {{jalink|En no Gyoja}}, and the shrine became a center of shugendo-shinto in the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}}. The current priests are said to descend from Gyoman Daimyojin. In {{jalink|1871|Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it was listed as gosha under the modern ranking system, and in {{jalink|1927|Q18110}} (Showa 2) it became kensha. In {{jalink|1935|Q18658}} (Showa 10), a survey by the Interior Ministry's Shrine Bureau designated it the head shrine ("Chigi Sarutahiko Daihongu") for about 2,000 Sarutahiko shrines nationwide. In practice, only a small number of those shrines have direct ties with this shrine. Meanwhile, shrines called "{{jalink|Tsubaki Shrine|Q11541039}}" enshrining "Tsubaki Daimyojin" exist in many regions. In {{jalink|1987|Q2429}} (Showa 62), it founded Tsubaki America Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America|Q840967}} in Washington State), beginning active promotion of {{jalink|Shinto|Q812767}} in the United States. Its treasures include a lion head said to have been dedicated by {{jalink|Kibi no Makibi|Q1063276}}, and the shrine is also considered a birthplace shrine of {{jalink|shishimai|Q713866}} lion dance. === Subsidiary shrines === === Betsugu === [[画像:Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Betsugu: Tsubakigishi Shrine]] * '''Tsubakigishi Shrine''' - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Futodama-no-Mikoto, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto * Okunomiya Iwaza (summit of {{jalink|Nyudogatake}}) - deities: Amaterasu, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Sarutahiko Ōkami * Ogishi Ōkami Shrine (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Takamimusubi-no-kami * Aishoi Shrine (Mizusawa-Noda-cho, Yokkaichi) - deity: Aishoi Daigongen * Ishi Ōkami (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Amaterasu === Massha === ==== Precinct massha ==== [[画像:椿大神社 - 土公神陵.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Doko Shinryo]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 御舟磐座2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Mifune Iwaza]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Matsushita Konosuke Shrine]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 鈴松庵2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Tea room Suzumatsu-an]] * Takayama Doko no Kami Mausoleum - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Mifune Iwaza - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Tado Shrine - deity: Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto * Gokoku Shrine - enshrined spirits of the war dead * Matsushita Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Konosuke Matsushita|Q252785|Matsushita Konosuke-no-Mikoto}} * Koryujin Shrine - deities: Golden Dragon, White Dragon, Black Dragon ==== Outside-precinct massha ==== * Tsubaki Ichinomiya Shrine (Tsubaki Ichinomiya-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Sarutahiko Ōkami * Kanno Shinmei Shrine (Kanno, Yamamoto-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Amaterasu, Sarutahiko Ōkami === Festivals === * Shishi Shin-go Kito ritual: Feb 11 - Apr 12 (once every 3 years) ** In years of Ox, Dragon, Sheep, and Dog ** First dance on Feb 11; concluding dance in precinct on Apr 12 * Setsubun Festival: day of Setsubun in February * Kinen-sai / Great purification festival: Feb 21 * Spring annual grand festival ** Spring grand festival: Apr 11 (dedication of Kyoto {{jalink|Kongo school|Q11646539}} ritual nō "Uzume") ** Kōsha grand festival: Apr 11-12 * Koryujin Shrine grand festival: early May * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya spring grand festival: May 12 * Rice-planting festival: last Sunday of May * Summer purification: Jun 30 * Summer festival: Aug 1 * Autumn annual grand festival ** Eve festival: Oct 10 ** Main shrine grand festival: Oct 11 ** Betsugu grand festival: Oct 12 * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya autumn grand festival: Nov 12 * Niiname Festival: Dec 1 * New Year's Eve great purification: Dec 31 === Visitor information === === Address === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}} Yamamoto-cho Otabi 1871 === Access === ==== Bus ==== * From {{jalink|Yokkaichi Station|Q6414379|Kintetsu Yokkaichi Station}} / {{jalink|Yokkaichi Station|Q5358995|JR Yokkaichi Station}}, take {{jalink|Mie Kotsu|Q6843516}} bus (bound for Tsubaki Grand Shrine), get off at final stop. * From Kintetsu {{jalink|Hiratacho Station|Q5769516}} / JR {{jalink|Kabase Station|Q5358774}}, take Suzuka community bus (C-BUS, Tsubaki-Hirata line loop), get off at "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" stop. ==== Private car ==== * About 5 km from {{jalink|Suzuka IC|Q11649468}} on the {{jalink|Higashi-Meihan Expressway|Q5753692}} * (ETC vehicles only) about 2 km from {{jalink|Suzuka PA Smart IC|Q11649477}} on the {{jalink|Shin-Meishin Expressway|Q7497118}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine|Q11643733}} - another shrine claiming Ichinomiya of Ise Province * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine|Q705303}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} * [http://www.tsubaki.or.jp/ Tsubaki Grand Shrine] (official website) ** {{Instagram|tsubakiokamiyashiro_official}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきおおかみやしろ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]]

Yashiro}} [[EN:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[FA:معبد بزرگ تسوباکی]] [[FR:Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro]] [[ID:Kuil Agung Tsubaki]] [[JA:椿大神社]] [[RU:Цубаки-оками-ясиро]] [[ZH:椿大神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3541617}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Grand Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsubaki-shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden of Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}} Otabi 1871, Yamamoto-cho |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|52|N|136|27|6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|kensha}}
{{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} |創建 = (tradition) 27th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |別名 = Sarutahiko Daihongu |例祭 = {{jalink|October 11|Q2927}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=52|lon_deg=136|lon_min=27|lon_sec=6|label=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|position=right|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' (つばきおおかみやしろ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and the {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}, and today it is a {{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} of {{jalink|Jinja Honcho|Q908077}}. In Mie Prefecture it ranks third in number of worshipers after {{jalink|Ise Jingu|Q687168}} and {{jalink|Futami Okitama Shrine|Q64969614}}; in 2013 it had 1,455,000 visitors{{Cite web|和書|publisher=Mie Prefecture Employment and Economy Dept., Tourism and International Bureau, Tourism Policy Division|url=http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|title=Visitor counts by tourism site category|accessdate=2015-01-25|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150124151905/http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|archivedate=2015-01-24}}. It is also called '''Sarutahiko Daihongu''', and is regarded as the head shrine of shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami. === Enshrined deities === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}}''' ; Co-enshrined deities * {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848}} - mother deity of Ninigi-no-Mikoto ; Associated deities * {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} - consort deity of Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} ; Forward seat * Gyoman Daimyojin === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 27th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}, by an oracle to {{jalink|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732}}, a shrine building was constructed near the tomb of Sarutahiko Ōkami as "the shrine of Michiwake Ōkami." The first historical appearance is in the June 17, {{jalink|748|Q23621}} ({{jalink|Tenpyo|Q1199879}} 20) ''Daian-ji Garan Engi narabi ni Ryuki Shizaicho''. This shrine is generally identified with "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' and as the ichinomiya of Ise Province. Tradition says Gyoman Daimyojin, a descendant of Sarutahiko Ōkami, founded {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} and guided {{jalink|En no Gyoja}}, and the shrine became a center of shugendo-shinto in the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}}. The current priests are said to descend from Gyoman Daimyojin. In {{jalink|1871|Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it was listed as gosha under the modern ranking system, and in {{jalink|1927|Q18110}} (Showa 2) it became kensha. In {{jalink|1935|Q18658}} (Showa 10), a survey by the Interior Ministry's Shrine Bureau designated it the head shrine ("Chigi Sarutahiko Daihongu") for about 2,000 Sarutahiko shrines nationwide. In practice, only a small number of those shrines have direct ties with this shrine. Meanwhile, shrines called "{{jalink|Tsubaki Shrine|Q11541039}}" enshrining "Tsubaki Daimyojin" exist in many regions. In {{jalink|1987|Q2429}} (Showa 62), it founded Tsubaki America Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America|Q840967}} in Washington State), beginning active promotion of {{jalink|Shinto|Q812767}} in the United States. Its treasures include a lion head said to have been dedicated by {{jalink|Kibi no Makibi|Q1063276}}, and the shrine is also considered a birthplace shrine of {{jalink|shishimai|Q713866}} lion dance. === Subsidiary shrines === === Betsugu === [[画像:Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Betsugu: Tsubakigishi Shrine]] * '''Tsubakigishi Shrine''' - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Futodama-no-Mikoto, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto * Okunomiya Iwaza (summit of {{jalink|Nyudogatake}}) - deities: Amaterasu, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Sarutahiko Ōkami * Ogishi Ōkami Shrine (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Takamimusubi-no-kami * Aishoi Shrine (Mizusawa-Noda-cho, Yokkaichi) - deity: Aishoi Daigongen * Ishi Ōkami (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Amaterasu === Massha === ==== Precinct massha ==== [[画像:椿大神社 - 土公神陵.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Doko Shinryo]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 御舟磐座2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Mifune Iwaza]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Matsushita Konosuke Shrine]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 鈴松庵2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Tea room Suzumatsu-an]] * Takayama Doko no Kami Mausoleum - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Mifune Iwaza - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Tado Shrine - deity: Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto * Gokoku Shrine - enshrined spirits of the war dead * Matsushita Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Konosuke Matsushita|Q252785|Matsushita Konosuke-no-Mikoto}} * Koryujin Shrine - deities: Golden Dragon, White Dragon, Black Dragon ==== Outside-precinct massha ==== * Tsubaki Ichinomiya Shrine (Tsubaki Ichinomiya-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Sarutahiko Ōkami * Kanno Shinmei Shrine (Kanno, Yamamoto-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Amaterasu, Sarutahiko Ōkami === Festivals === * Shishi Shin-go Kito ritual: Feb 11 - Apr 12 (once every 3 years) ** In years of Ox, Dragon, Sheep, and Dog ** First dance on Feb 11; concluding dance in precinct on Apr 12 * Setsubun Festival: day of Setsubun in February * Kinen-sai / Great purification festival: Feb 21 * Spring annual grand festival ** Spring grand festival: Apr 11 (dedication of Kyoto {{jalink|Kongo school|Q11646539}} ritual nō "Uzume") ** Kōsha grand festival: Apr 11-12 * Koryujin Shrine grand festival: early May * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya spring grand festival: May 12 * Rice-planting festival: last Sunday of May * Summer purification: Jun 30 * Summer festival: Aug 1 * Autumn annual grand festival ** Eve festival: Oct 10 ** Main shrine grand festival: Oct 11 ** Betsugu grand festival: Oct 12 * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya autumn grand festival: Nov 12 * Niiname Festival: Dec 1 * New Year's Eve great purification: Dec 31 === Visitor information === === Address === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}} Yamamoto-cho Otabi 1871 === Access === ==== Bus ==== * From {{jalink|Yokkaichi Station|Q6414379|Kintetsu Yokkaichi Station}} / {{jalink|Yokkaichi Station|Q5358995|JR Yokkaichi Station}}, take {{jalink|Mie Kotsu|Q6843516}} bus (bound for Tsubaki Grand Shrine), get off at final stop. * From Kintetsu {{jalink|Hiratacho Station|Q5769516}} / JR {{jalink|Kabase Station|Q5358774}}, take Suzuka community bus (C-BUS, Tsubaki-Hirata line loop), get off at "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" stop. ==== Private car ==== * About 5 km from {{jalink|Suzuka IC|Q11649468}} on the {{jalink|Higashi-Meihan Expressway|Q5753692}} * (ETC vehicles only) about 2 km from {{jalink|Suzuka PA Smart IC|Q11649477}} on the {{jalink|Shin-Meishin Expressway|Q7497118}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine|Q11643733}} - another shrine claiming Ichinomiya of Ise Province * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine|Q705303}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} * [http://www.tsubaki.or.jp/ Tsubaki Grand Shrine] (official website) ** {{Instagram|tsubakiokamiyashiro_official}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきおおかみやしろ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

Yashiro}} [[EN:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[FA:معبد بزرگ تسوباکی]] [[FR:Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro]] [[ID:Kuil Agung Tsubaki]] [[JA:椿大神社]] [[RU:Цубаки-оками-ясиро]] [[ZH:椿大神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3541617}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Grand Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsubaki-shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden of Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}} Otabi 1871, Yamamoto-cho |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|52|N|136|27|6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|kensha}}
{{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} |創建 = (tradition) 27th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |別名 = Sarutahiko Daihongu |例祭 = {{jalink|October 11|Q2927}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=52|lon_deg=136|lon_min=27|lon_sec=6|label=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|position=right|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' (つばきおおかみやしろ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. === Overview === It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and the {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}} of {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}, and today it is a {{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} of {{jalink|Jinja Honcho|Q908077}}. In Mie Prefecture it ranks third in number of worshipers after {{jalink|Ise Jingu|Q687168}} and {{jalink|Futami Okitama Shrine|Q64969614}}; in 2013 it had 1,455,000 visitors{{Cite web|和書|publisher=Mie Prefecture Employment and Economy Dept., Tourism and International Bureau, Tourism Policy Division|url=http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|title=Visitor counts by tourism site category|accessdate=2015-01-25|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150124151905/http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|archivedate=2015-01-24}}. It is also called '''Sarutahiko Daihongu''', and is regarded as the head shrine of shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami. === Enshrined deities === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko Ōkami}}''' ; Co-enshrined deities * {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848}} - mother deity of Ninigi-no-Mikoto ; Associated deities * {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} - consort deity of Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} ; Forward seat * Gyoman Daimyojin === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 27th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}, by an oracle to {{jalink|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732}}, a shrine building was constructed near the tomb of Sarutahiko Ōkami as "the shrine of Michiwake Ōkami." The first historical appearance is in the June 17, {{jalink|748|Q23621}} ({{jalink|Tenpyo|Q1199879}} 20) ''Daian-ji Garan Engi narabi ni Ryuki Shizaicho''. This shrine is generally identified with "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' and as the ichinomiya of Ise Province. Tradition says Gyoman Daimyojin, a descendant of Sarutahiko Ōkami, founded {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} and guided {{jalink|En no Gyoja}}, and the shrine became a center of shugendo-shinto in the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}}. The current priests are said to descend from Gyoman Daimyojin. In {{jalink|1871|Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it was listed as gosha under the modern ranking system, and in {{jalink|1927|Q18110}} (Showa 2) it became kensha. In {{jalink|1935|Q18658}} (Showa 10), a survey by the Interior Ministry's Shrine Bureau designated it the head shrine ("Chigi Sarutahiko Daihongu") for about 2,000 Sarutahiko shrines nationwide. In practice, only a small number of those shrines have direct ties with this shrine. Meanwhile, shrines called "{{jalink|Tsubaki Shrine|Q11541039}}" enshrining "Tsubaki Daimyojin" exist in many regions. In {{jalink|1987|Q2429}} (Showa 62), it founded Tsubaki America Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America|Q840967}} in Washington State), beginning active promotion of {{jalink|Shinto|Q812767}} in the United States. Its treasures include a lion head said to have been dedicated by {{jalink|Kibi no Makibi|Q1063276}}, and the shrine is also considered a birthplace shrine of {{jalink|shishimai|Q713866}} lion dance. === Subsidiary shrines === === Betsugu === [[画像:Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Betsugu: Tsubakigishi Shrine]] * '''Tsubakigishi Shrine''' - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Futodama-no-Mikoto, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto * Okunomiya Iwaza (summit of {{jalink|Nyudogatake}}) - deities: Amaterasu, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Sarutahiko Ōkami * Ogishi Ōkami Shrine (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Takamimusubi-no-kami * Aishoi Shrine (Mizusawa-Noda-cho, Yokkaichi) - deity: Aishoi Daigongen * Ishi Ōkami (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Amaterasu === Massha === ==== Precinct massha ==== [[画像:椿大神社 - 土公神陵.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Doko Shinryo]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 御舟磐座2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Mifune Iwaza]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Matsushita Konosuke Shrine]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 鈴松庵2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Tea room Suzumatsu-an]] * Takayama Doko no Kami Mausoleum - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Mifune Iwaza - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Tado Shrine - deity: Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto * Gokoku Shrine - enshrined spirits of the war dead * Matsushita Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Konosuke Matsushita|Q252785|Matsushita Konosuke-no-Mikoto}} * Koryujin Shrine - deities: Golden Dragon, White Dragon, Black Dragon ==== Outside-precinct massha ==== * Tsubaki Ichinomiya Shrine (Tsubaki Ichinomiya-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Sarutahiko Ōkami * Kanno Shinmei Shrine (Kanno, Yamamoto-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Amaterasu, Sarutahiko Ōkami === Festivals === * Shishi Shin-go Kito ritual: Feb 11 - Apr 12 (once every 3 years) ** In years of Ox, Dragon, Sheep, and Dog ** First dance on Feb 11; concluding dance in precinct on Apr 12 * Setsubun Festival: day of Setsubun in February * Kinen-sai / Great purification festival: Feb 21 * Spring annual grand festival ** Spring grand festival: Apr 11 (dedication of Kyoto {{jalink|Kongo school|Q11646539}} ritual nō "Uzume") ** Kōsha grand festival: Apr 11-12 * Koryujin Shrine grand festival: early May * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya spring grand festival: May 12 * Rice-planting festival: last Sunday of May * Summer purification: Jun 30 * Summer festival: Aug 1 * Autumn annual grand festival ** Eve festival: Oct 10 ** Main shrine grand festival: Oct 11 ** Betsugu grand festival: Oct 12 * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya autumn grand festival: Nov 12 * Niiname Festival: Dec 1 * New Year's Eve great purification: Dec 31 === Visitor information === === Address === * {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Suzuka|Q207311}} Yamamoto-cho Otabi 1871 === Access === ==== Bus ==== * From {{jalink|Yokkaichi Station|Q6414379|Kintetsu Yokkaichi Station}} / {{jalink|Yokkaichi Station|Q5358995|JR Yokkaichi Station}}, take {{jalink|Mie Kotsu|Q6843516}} bus (bound for Tsubaki Grand Shrine), get off at final stop. * From Kintetsu {{jalink|Hiratacho Station|Q5769516}} / JR {{jalink|Kabase Station|Q5358774}}, take Suzuka community bus (C-BUS, Tsubaki-Hirata line loop), get off at "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" stop. ==== Private car ==== * About 5 km from {{jalink|Suzuka IC|Q11649468}} on the {{jalink|Higashi-Meihan Expressway|Q5753692}} * (ETC vehicles only) about 2 km from {{jalink|Suzuka PA Smart IC|Q11649477}} on the {{jalink|Shin-Meishin Expressway|Q7497118}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{jalink|Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine|Q11643733}} - another shrine claiming Ichinomiya of Ise Province * {{jalink|Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141}} * {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine|Q705303}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} * [http://www.tsubaki.or.jp/ Tsubaki Grand Shrine] (official website) ** {{Instagram|tsubakiokamiyashiro_official}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきおおかみやしろ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Grand Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsubaki-shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden of Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}} Otabi 1871, Yamamoto-cho |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|52|N|136|27|6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}
former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} |創建 = (tradition) 27th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} |別名 = Sarutahiko Daihongu |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 11|Q2927|WD=Q2927}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=52|lon_deg=136|lon_min=27|lon_sec=6|label=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|position=right|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' (つばきおおかみやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system}} it was a {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}, and today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} of {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. In Mie Prefecture it ranks third in number of worshipers after {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|WD=Q687168}} and {{ill|lt=Futami Okitama Shrine|Q64969614|WD=Q64969614}}; in 2013 it had 1,455,000 visitors{{Cite web|和書|publisher=Mie Prefecture Employment and Economy Dept., Tourism and International Bureau, Tourism Policy Division|url=http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|title=Visitor counts by tourism site category|accessdate=2015-01-25|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150124151905/http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|archivedate=2015-01-24}}. It is also called '''Sarutahiko Daihongu''', and is regarded as the head shrine of shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami. === Enshrined deities === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}''' ; Co-enshrined deities * {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848}} - mother deity of Ninigi-no-Mikoto ; Associated deities * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} - consort deity of Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} ; Forward seat * Gyoman Daimyojin === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 27th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, by an oracle to {{ill|lt=Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}}, a shrine building was constructed near the tomb of Sarutahiko Ōkami as "the shrine of Michiwake Ōkami." The first historical appearance is in the June 17, {{ill|lt=748|Q23621|WD=Q23621}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 20) ''Daian-ji Garan Engi narabi ni Ryuki Shizaicho''. This shrine is generally identified with "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' and as the ichinomiya of Ise Province. Tradition says Gyoman Daimyojin, a descendant of Sarutahiko Ōkami, founded {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875}} and guided {{ill||lt=En no Gyoja|UNKNOWN}}, and the shrine became a center of shugendo-shinto in the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}. The current priests are said to descend from Gyoman Daimyojin. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it was listed as gosha under the modern ranking system, and in {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|WD=Q18110}} (Showa 2) it became kensha. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} (Showa 10), a survey by the Interior Ministry's Shrine Bureau designated it the head shrine ("Chigi Sarutahiko Daihongu") for about 2,000 Sarutahiko shrines nationwide. In practice, only a small number of those shrines have direct ties with this shrine. Meanwhile, shrines called "{{ill|lt=Tsubaki Shrine|Q11541039|WD=Q11541039}}" enshrining "Tsubaki Daimyojin" exist in many regions. In {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}} (Showa 62), it founded Tsubaki America Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America|Q840967|WD=Q840967}} in Washington State), beginning active promotion of {{ill|lt=Shinto|Q812767|WD=Q812767}} in the United States. Its treasures include a lion head said to have been dedicated by {{ill|lt=Kibi no Makibi|Q1063276|WD=Q1063276}}, and the shrine is also considered a birthplace shrine of {{ill|lt=shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} lion dance. === Subsidiary shrines === === Betsugu === [[画像:Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Betsugu: Tsubakigishi Shrine]] * '''Tsubakigishi Shrine''' - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Futodama-no-Mikoto, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto * Okunomiya Iwaza (summit of {{ill||lt=Nyudogatake|UNKNOWN}}) - deities: Amaterasu, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Sarutahiko Ōkami * Ogishi Ōkami Shrine (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Takamimusubi-no-kami * Aishoi Shrine (Mizusawa-Noda-cho, Yokkaichi) - deity: Aishoi Daigongen * Ishi Ōkami (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Amaterasu === Massha === ==== Precinct massha ==== [[画像:椿大神社 - 土公神陵.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Doko Shinryo]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 御舟磐座2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Mifune Iwaza]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Matsushita Konosuke Shrine]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 鈴松庵2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Tea room Suzumatsu-an]] * Takayama Doko no Kami Mausoleum - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Mifune Iwaza - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Tado Shrine - deity: Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto * Gokoku Shrine - enshrined spirits of the war dead * Matsushita Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Konosuke Matsushita|Q252785|WD=Q252785|lt=Matsushita Konosuke-no-Mikoto}} * Koryujin Shrine - deities: Golden Dragon, White Dragon, Black Dragon ==== Outside-precinct massha ==== * Tsubaki Ichinomiya Shrine (Tsubaki Ichinomiya-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Sarutahiko Ōkami * Kanno Shinmei Shrine (Kanno, Yamamoto-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Amaterasu, Sarutahiko Ōkami === Festivals === * Shishi Shin-go Kito ritual: Feb 11 - Apr 12 (once every 3 years) ** In years of Ox, Dragon, Sheep, and Dog ** First dance on Feb 11; concluding dance in precinct on Apr 12 * Setsubun Festival: day of Setsubun in February * Kinen-sai / Great purification festival: Feb 21 * Spring annual grand festival ** Spring grand festival: Apr 11 (dedication of Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kongo school|Q11646539|WD=Q11646539}} ritual nō "Uzume") ** Kōsha grand festival: Apr 11-12 * Koryujin Shrine grand festival: early May * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya spring grand festival: May 12 * Rice-planting festival: last Sunday of May * Summer purification: Jun 30 * Summer festival: Aug 1 * Autumn annual grand festival ** Eve festival: Oct 10 ** Main shrine grand festival: Oct 11 ** Betsugu grand festival: Oct 12 * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya autumn grand festival: Nov 12 * Niiname Festival: Dec 1 * New Year's Eve great purification: Dec 31 === Visitor information === === Address === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}} Yamamoto-cho Otabi 1871 === Access === ==== Bus ==== * From {{ill|lt=Yokkaichi Station|Q6414379|WD=Q6414379|lt=Kintetsu Yokkaichi Station}} / {{ill|lt=Yokkaichi Station|Q5358995|WD=Q5358995|lt=JR Yokkaichi Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Mie Kotsu|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516}} bus (bound for Tsubaki Grand Shrine), get off at final stop. * From Kintetsu {{ill|lt=Hiratacho Station|Q5769516|WD=Q5769516}} / JR {{ill|lt=Kabase Station|Q5358774|WD=Q5358774}}, take Suzuka community bus (C-BUS, Tsubaki-Hirata line loop), get off at "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" stop. ==== Private car ==== * About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Suzuka IC|Q11649468|WD=Q11649468}} on the {{ill|lt=Higashi-Meihan Expressway|Q5753692|WD=Q5753692}} * (ETC vehicles only) about 2 km from {{ill|lt=Suzuka PA Smart IC|Q11649477|WD=Q11649477}} on the {{ill|lt=Shin-Meishin Expressway|Q7497118|WD=Q7497118}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine|Q11643733|WD=Q11643733}} - another shrine claiming Ichinomiya of Ise Province * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141}} * {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} * [http://www.tsubaki.or.jp/ Tsubaki Grand Shrine] (official website) ** {{Instagram|tsubakiokamiyashiro_official}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきおおかみやしろ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Grand Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsubaki-shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden of Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}} Otabi 1871, Yamamoto-cho |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|52|N|136|27|6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} |創建 = (tradition) 27th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} |別名 = Sarutahiko Daihongu |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 11|Q2927|WD=Q2927}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=52|lon_deg=136|lon_min=27|lon_sec=6|label=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|position=right|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' (つばきおおかみやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}, and today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} of {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. In Mie Prefecture it ranks third in number of worshipers after {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|WD=Q687168}} and {{ill|lt=Futami Okitama Shrine|Q64969614|WD=Q64969614}}; in 2013 it had 1,455,000 visitors{{Cite web|和書|publisher=Mie Prefecture Employment and Economy Dept., Tourism and International Bureau, Tourism Policy Division|url=http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|title=Visitor counts by tourism site category|accessdate=2015-01-25|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150124151905/http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|archivedate=2015-01-24}}. It is also called '''Sarutahiko Daihongu''', and is regarded as the head shrine of shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami. === Enshrined deities === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}''' ; Co-enshrined deities * {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848}} - mother deity of Ninigi-no-Mikoto ; Associated deities * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} - consort deity of Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} ; Forward seat * Gyoman Daimyojin === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 27th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, by an oracle to {{ill|lt=Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}}, a shrine building was constructed near the tomb of Sarutahiko Ōkami as "the shrine of Michiwake Ōkami." The first historical appearance is in the June 17, {{ill|lt=748|Q23621|WD=Q23621}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 20) ''Daian-ji Garan Engi narabi ni Ryuki Shizaicho''. This shrine is generally identified with "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' and as the ichinomiya of Ise Province. Tradition says Gyoman Daimyojin, a descendant of Sarutahiko Ōkami, founded {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875}} and guided {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyoja|UNKNOWN}}, and the shrine became a center of shugendo-shinto in the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}. The current priests are said to descend from Gyoman Daimyojin. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it was listed as gosha under the modern ranking system, and in {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|WD=Q18110}} (Showa 2) it became kensha. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} (Showa 10), a survey by the Interior Ministry's Shrine Bureau designated it the head shrine ("Chigi Sarutahiko Daihongu") for about 2,000 Sarutahiko shrines nationwide. In practice, only a small number of those shrines have direct ties with this shrine. Meanwhile, shrines called "{{ill|lt=Tsubaki Shrine|Q11541039|WD=Q11541039}}" enshrining "Tsubaki Daimyojin" exist in many regions. In {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}} (Showa 62), it founded Tsubaki America Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America|Q840967|WD=Q840967}} in Washington State), beginning active promotion of {{ill|lt=Shinto|Q812767|WD=Q812767}} in the United States. Its treasures include a lion head said to have been dedicated by {{ill|lt=Kibi no Makibi|Q1063276|WD=Q1063276}}, and the shrine is also considered a birthplace shrine of {{ill|lt=shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} lion dance. === Subsidiary shrines === === Betsugu === [[画像:Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Betsugu: Tsubakigishi Shrine]] * '''Tsubakigishi Shrine''' - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Futodama-no-Mikoto, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto * Okunomiya Iwaza (summit of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nyudogatake|UNKNOWN}}) - deities: Amaterasu, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Sarutahiko Ōkami * Ogishi Ōkami Shrine (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Takamimusubi-no-kami * Aishoi Shrine (Mizusawa-Noda-cho, Yokkaichi) - deity: Aishoi Daigongen * Ishi Ōkami (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Amaterasu === Massha === ==== Precinct massha ==== [[画像:椿大神社 - 土公神陵.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Doko Shinryo]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 御舟磐座2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Mifune Iwaza]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Matsushita Konosuke Shrine]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 鈴松庵2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Tea room Suzumatsu-an]] * Takayama Doko no Kami Mausoleum - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Mifune Iwaza - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Tado Shrine - deity: Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto * Gokoku Shrine - enshrined spirits of the war dead * Matsushita Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Konosuke Matsushita|Q252785|WD=Q252785|lt=Matsushita Konosuke-no-Mikoto}} * Koryujin Shrine - deities: Golden Dragon, White Dragon, Black Dragon ==== Outside-precinct massha ==== * Tsubaki Ichinomiya Shrine (Tsubaki Ichinomiya-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Sarutahiko Ōkami * Kanno Shinmei Shrine (Kanno, Yamamoto-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Amaterasu, Sarutahiko Ōkami === Festivals === * Shishi Shin-go Kito ritual: Feb 11 - Apr 12 (once every 3 years) ** In years of Ox, Dragon, Sheep, and Dog ** First dance on Feb 11; concluding dance in precinct on Apr 12 * Setsubun Festival: day of Setsubun in February * Kinen-sai / Great purification festival: Feb 21 * Spring annual grand festival ** Spring grand festival: Apr 11 (dedication of Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kongo school|Q11646539|WD=Q11646539}} ritual nō "Uzume") ** Kōsha grand festival: Apr 11-12 * Koryujin Shrine grand festival: early May * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya spring grand festival: May 12 * Rice-planting festival: last Sunday of May * Summer purification: Jun 30 * Summer festival: Aug 1 * Autumn annual grand festival ** Eve festival: Oct 10 ** Main shrine grand festival: Oct 11 ** Betsugu grand festival: Oct 12 * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya autumn grand festival: Nov 12 * Niiname Festival: Dec 1 * New Year's Eve great purification: Dec 31 === Visitor information === === Address === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}} Yamamoto-cho Otabi 1871 === Access === ==== Bus ==== * From {{ill|lt=Yokkaichi Station|Q6414379|WD=Q6414379|lt=Kintetsu Yokkaichi Station}} / {{ill|lt=Yokkaichi Station|Q5358995|WD=Q5358995|lt=JR Yokkaichi Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Mie Kotsu|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516}} bus (bound for Tsubaki Grand Shrine), get off at final stop. * From Kintetsu {{ill|lt=Hiratacho Station|Q5769516|WD=Q5769516}} / JR {{ill|lt=Kabase Station|Q5358774|WD=Q5358774}}, take Suzuka community bus (C-BUS, Tsubaki-Hirata line loop), get off at "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" stop. ==== Private car ==== * About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Suzuka IC|Q11649468|WD=Q11649468}} on the {{ill|lt=Higashi-Meihan Expressway|Q5753692|WD=Q5753692}} * (ETC vehicles only) about 2 km from {{ill|lt=Suzuka PA Smart IC|Q11649477|WD=Q11649477}} on the {{ill|lt=Shin-Meishin Expressway|Q7497118|WD=Q7497118}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine|Q11643733|WD=Q11643733}} - another shrine claiming Ichinomiya of Ise Province * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141}} * {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} * [http://www.tsubaki.or.jp/ Tsubaki Grand Shrine] (official website) ** {{Instagram|tsubakiokamiyashiro_official}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきおおかみやしろ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Yashiro}} [[EN:Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] [[FA:معبد بزرگ تسوباکی]] [[FR:Tsubaki Ōkami-yashiro]] [[ID:Kuil Agung Tsubaki]] [[JA:椿大神社]] [[RU:Цубаки-оками-ясиро]] [[ZH:椿大神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3541617}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Grand Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsubaki-shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden of Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}} Otabi 1871, Yamamoto-cho |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|52|N|136|27|6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} |創建 = (tradition) 27th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} |別名 = Sarutahiko Daihongu |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 11|Q2927|WD=Q2927}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=52|lon_deg=136|lon_min=27|lon_sec=6|label=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|position=right|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' (つばきおおかみやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} of {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. In Mie Prefecture it ranks third in number of worshipers after {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|WD=Q687168}} and {{ill|lt=Futami Okitama Shrine|Q64969614|WD=Q64969614}}; in 2013 it had 1,455,000 visitors{{Cite web|和書|publisher=Mie Prefecture Employment and Economy Dept., Tourism and International Bureau, Tourism Policy Division|url=http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|title=Visitor counts by tourism site category|accessdate=2015-01-25|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150124151905/http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|archivedate=2015-01-24}}. It is also called '''Sarutahiko Daihongu''', and is regarded as the head shrine of shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami. === Enshrined deities === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}''' ; Co-enshrined deities * {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848}} - mother deity of Ninigi-no-Mikoto ; Associated deities * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} - consort deity of Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} ; Forward seat * Gyoman Daimyojin === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 27th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, by an oracle to {{ill|lt=Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}}, a shrine building was constructed near the tomb of Sarutahiko Ōkami as "the shrine of Michiwake Ōkami." The first historical appearance is in the June 17, {{ill|lt=748|Q23621|WD=Q23621}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 20) ''Daian-ji Garan Engi narabi ni Ryuki Shizaicho''. This shrine is generally identified with "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' and as the ichinomiya of Ise Province. Tradition says Gyoman Daimyojin, a descendant of Sarutahiko Ōkami, founded {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875}} and guided {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyoja|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the shrine became a center of shugendo-shinto in the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}. The current priests are said to descend from Gyoman Daimyojin. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it was listed as gosha under the modern ranking system, and in {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|WD=Q18110}} (Showa 2) it became kensha. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} (Showa 10), a survey by the Interior Ministry's Shrine Bureau designated it the head shrine ("Chigi Sarutahiko Daihongu") for about 2,000 Sarutahiko shrines nationwide. In practice, only a small number of those shrines have direct ties with this shrine. Meanwhile, shrines called "{{ill|lt=Tsubaki Shrine|Q11541039|WD=Q11541039}}" enshrining "Tsubaki Daimyojin" exist in many regions. In {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}} (Showa 62), it founded Tsubaki America Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America|Q840967|WD=Q840967}} in Washington State), beginning active promotion of {{ill|lt=Shinto|Q812767|WD=Q812767}} in the United States. Its treasures include a lion head said to have been dedicated by {{ill|lt=Kibi no Makibi|Q1063276|WD=Q1063276}}, and the shrine is also considered a birthplace shrine of {{ill|lt=shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} lion dance. === Subsidiary shrines === === Betsugu === [[画像:Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Betsugu: Tsubakigishi Shrine]] * '''Tsubakigishi Shrine''' - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Futodama-no-Mikoto, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto * Okunomiya Iwaza (summit of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nyudogatake|1=UNKNOWN}}) - deities: Amaterasu, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Sarutahiko Ōkami * Ogishi Ōkami Shrine (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Takamimusubi-no-kami * Aishoi Shrine (Mizusawa-Noda-cho, Yokkaichi) - deity: Aishoi Daigongen * Ishi Ōkami (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Amaterasu === Massha === ==== Precinct massha ==== [[画像:椿大神社 - 土公神陵.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Doko Shinryo]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 御舟磐座2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Mifune Iwaza]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Matsushita Konosuke Shrine]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 鈴松庵2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Tea room Suzumatsu-an]] * Takayama Doko no Kami Mausoleum - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Mifune Iwaza - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Tado Shrine - deity: Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto * Gokoku Shrine - enshrined spirits of the war dead * Matsushita Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Konosuke Matsushita|Q252785|WD=Q252785|lt=Matsushita Konosuke-no-Mikoto}} * Koryujin Shrine - deities: Golden Dragon, White Dragon, Black Dragon ==== Outside-precinct massha ==== * Tsubaki Ichinomiya Shrine (Tsubaki Ichinomiya-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Sarutahiko Ōkami * Kanno Shinmei Shrine (Kanno, Yamamoto-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Amaterasu, Sarutahiko Ōkami === Festivals === * Shishi Shin-go Kito ritual: Feb 11 - Apr 12 (once every 3 years) ** In years of Ox, Dragon, Sheep, and Dog ** First dance on Feb 11; concluding dance in precinct on Apr 12 * Setsubun Festival: day of Setsubun in February * Kinen-sai / Great purification festival: Feb 21 * Spring annual grand festival ** Spring grand festival: Apr 11 (dedication of Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kongo school|Q11646539|WD=Q11646539}} ritual nō "Uzume") ** Kōsha grand festival: Apr 11-12 * Koryujin Shrine grand festival: early May * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya spring grand festival: May 12 * Rice-planting festival: last Sunday of May * Summer purification: Jun 30 * Summer festival: Aug 1 * Autumn annual grand festival ** Eve festival: Oct 10 ** Main shrine grand festival: Oct 11 ** Betsugu grand festival: Oct 12 * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya autumn grand festival: Nov 12 * Niiname Festival: Dec 1 * New Year's Eve great purification: Dec 31 === Visitor information === === Address === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}} Yamamoto-cho Otabi 1871 === Access === ==== Bus ==== * From {{ill|lt=Yokkaichi Station|Q6414379|WD=Q6414379|lt=Kintetsu Yokkaichi Station}} / {{ill|lt=Yokkaichi Station|Q5358995|WD=Q5358995|lt=JR Yokkaichi Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Mie Kotsu|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516}} bus (bound for Tsubaki Grand Shrine), get off at final stop. * From Kintetsu {{ill|lt=Hiratacho Station|Q5769516|WD=Q5769516}} / JR {{ill|lt=Kabase Station|Q5358774|WD=Q5358774}}, take Suzuka community bus (C-BUS, Tsubaki-Hirata line loop), get off at "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" stop. ==== Private car ==== * About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Suzuka IC|Q11649468|WD=Q11649468}} on the {{ill|lt=Higashi-Meihan Expressway|Q5753692|WD=Q5753692}} * (ETC vehicles only) about 2 km from {{ill|lt=Suzuka PA Smart IC|Q11649477|WD=Q11649477}} on the {{ill|lt=Shin-Meishin Expressway|Q7497118|WD=Q7497118}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine|Q11643733|WD=Q11643733}} - another shrine claiming Ichinomiya of Ise Province * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141}} * {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} * [http://www.tsubaki.or.jp/ Tsubaki Grand Shrine] (official website) ** {{Instagram|tsubakiokamiyashiro_official}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきおおかみやしろ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsubaki Grand Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsubaki-shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden of Tsubaki Grand Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}} Otabi 1871, Yamamoto-cho |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|52|N|136|27|6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} |創建 = (tradition) 27th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} |別名 = Sarutahiko Daihongu |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 11|Q2927|WD=Q2927}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=52|lon_deg=136|lon_min=27|lon_sec=6|label=Tsubaki Grand Shrine|position=right|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} '''Tsubaki Grand Shrine''' (つばきおおかみやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} of {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. In Mie Prefecture it ranks third in number of worshipers after {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|WD=Q687168}} and {{ill|lt=Futami Okitama Shrine|Q64969614|WD=Q64969614}}; in 2013 it had 1,455,000 visitors{{Cite web|和書|publisher=Mie Prefecture Employment and Economy Dept., Tourism and International Bureau, Tourism Policy Division|url=http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|title=Visitor counts by tourism site category|accessdate=2015-01-25|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150124151905/http://www.pref.mie.lg.jp/TOPICS/201405010921.pdf|archivedate=2015-01-24}}. It is also called '''Sarutahiko Daihongu''', and is regarded as the head shrine of shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami. === Enshrined deities === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}''' ; Co-enshrined deities * {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848}} - mother deity of Ninigi-no-Mikoto ; Associated deities * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} - consort deity of Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} ; Forward seat * Gyoman Daimyojin === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 27th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, by an oracle to {{ill|lt=Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}}, a shrine building was constructed near the tomb of Sarutahiko Ōkami as "the shrine of Michiwake Ōkami." The first historical appearance is in the June 17, {{ill|lt=748|Q23621|WD=Q23621}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 20) ''Daian-ji Garan Engi narabi ni Ryuki Shizaicho''. This shrine is generally identified with "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' and as the ichinomiya of Ise Province. Tradition says Gyoman Daimyojin, a descendant of Sarutahiko Ōkami, founded {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875}} and guided {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyoja|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the shrine became a center of shugendo-shinto in the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}. The current priests are said to descend from Gyoman Daimyojin. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it was listed as gosha under the modern ranking system, and in {{ill|lt=1927|Q18110|WD=Q18110}} (Showa 2) it became kensha. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} (Showa 10), a survey by the Interior Ministry's Shrine Bureau designated it the head shrine ("Chigi Sarutahiko Daihongu") for about 2,000 Sarutahiko shrines nationwide. In practice, only a small number of those shrines have direct ties with this shrine. Meanwhile, shrines called "{{ill|lt=Tsubaki Shrine|Q11541039|WD=Q11541039}}" enshrining "Tsubaki Daimyojin" exist in many regions. In {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}} (Showa 62), it founded Tsubaki America Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America|Q840967|WD=Q840967}} in Washington State), beginning active promotion of {{ill|lt=Shinto|Q812767|WD=Q812767}} in the United States. Its treasures include a lion head said to have been dedicated by {{ill|lt=Kibi no Makibi|Q1063276|WD=Q1063276}}, and the shrine is also considered a birthplace shrine of {{ill|lt=shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} lion dance. === Subsidiary shrines === === Betsugu === [[画像:Uzume-honguu shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Betsugu: Tsubakigishi Shrine]] * '''Tsubakigishi Shrine''' - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Futodama-no-Mikoto, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto * Okunomiya Iwaza (summit of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nyudogatake|1=UNKNOWN}}) - deities: Amaterasu, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Sarutahiko Ōkami * Ogishi Ōkami Shrine (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, Takamimusubi-no-kami * Aishoi Shrine (Mizusawa-Noda-cho, Yokkaichi) - deity: Aishoi Daigongen * Ishi Ōkami (Kogisu-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Amaterasu === Massha === ==== Precinct massha ==== [[画像:椿大神社 - 土公神陵.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Doko Shinryo]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 御舟磐座2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Mifune Iwaza]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 末社・松下幸之助社.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Matsushita Konosuke Shrine]] [[画像:椿大神社 - 鈴松庵2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Tea room Suzumatsu-an]] * Takayama Doko no Kami Mausoleum - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Mifune Iwaza - deities: Sarutahiko Ōkami, Ninigi-no-Mikoto, Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto * Tado Shrine - deity: Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto * Gokoku Shrine - enshrined spirits of the war dead * Matsushita Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Konosuke Matsushita|Q252785|WD=Q252785|lt=Matsushita Konosuke-no-Mikoto}} * Koryujin Shrine - deities: Golden Dragon, White Dragon, Black Dragon ==== Outside-precinct massha ==== * Tsubaki Ichinomiya Shrine (Tsubaki Ichinomiya-cho, Suzuka) - deity: Sarutahiko Ōkami * Kanno Shinmei Shrine (Kanno, Yamamoto-cho, Suzuka) - deities: Amaterasu, Sarutahiko Ōkami === Festivals === * Shishi Shin-go Kito ritual: Feb 11 - Apr 12 (once every 3 years) ** In years of Ox, Dragon, Sheep, and Dog ** First dance on Feb 11; concluding dance in precinct on Apr 12 * Setsubun Festival: day of Setsubun in February * Kinen-sai / Great purification festival: Feb 21 * Spring annual grand festival ** Spring grand festival: Apr 11 (dedication of Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kongo school|Q11646539|WD=Q11646539}} ritual nō "Uzume") ** Kōsha grand festival: Apr 11-12 * Koryujin Shrine grand festival: early May * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya spring grand festival: May 12 * Rice-planting festival: last Sunday of May * Summer purification: Jun 30 * Summer festival: Aug 1 * Autumn annual grand festival ** Eve festival: Oct 10 ** Main shrine grand festival: Oct 11 ** Betsugu grand festival: Oct 12 * Nyudogatake summit Okunomiya autumn grand festival: Nov 12 * Niiname Festival: Dec 1 * New Year's Eve great purification: Dec 31 === Visitor information === === Address === * {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|WD=Q207311}} Yamamoto-cho Otabi 1871 === Access === ==== Bus ==== * From {{ill|lt=Yokkaichi Station|Q6414379|WD=Q6414379|lt=Kintetsu Yokkaichi Station}} / {{ill|lt=Yokkaichi Station|Q5358995|WD=Q5358995|lt=JR Yokkaichi Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Mie Kotsu|Q6843516|WD=Q6843516}} bus (bound for Tsubaki Grand Shrine), get off at final stop. * From Kintetsu {{ill|lt=Hiratacho Station|Q5769516|WD=Q5769516}} / JR {{ill|lt=Kabase Station|Q5358774|WD=Q5358774}}, take Suzuka community bus (C-BUS, Tsubaki-Hirata line loop), get off at "Tsubaki Ōkamiyashiro" stop. ==== Private car ==== * About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Suzuka IC|Q11649468|WD=Q11649468}} on the {{ill|lt=Higashi-Meihan Expressway|Q5753692|WD=Q5753692}} * (ETC vehicles only) about 2 km from {{ill|lt=Suzuka PA Smart IC|Q11649477|WD=Q11649477}} on the {{ill|lt=Shin-Meishin Expressway|Q7497118|WD=Q7497118}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === ; Shrines enshrining Sarutahiko Ōkami * {{ill|lt=Tsubaki Shrine and Nakato Shrine|Q11643733|WD=Q11643733}} - another shrine claiming Ichinomiya of Ise Province * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|Q11572141|WD=Q11572141}} * {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsubaki Ōkami Yashiro}} * [http://www.tsubaki.or.jp/ Tsubaki Grand Shrine] (official website) ** {{Instagram|tsubakiokamiyashiro_official}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきおおかみやしろ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:伊勢国|社1]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
